title: Java Module Import
description: Import a whole module in one line with a module import declaration.
learningGoals: Import every package exported by a module with a module import declaration (JEP 511).
Recall that compact source files already import java.base, making this example explicit but redundant.

completionChecks.prompt: What does import module java.base make visible?
choices: Every package in every module on the module path
Public types from packages exported by java.base
completionChecks.explanation: A module import declaration imports the public top-level types from packages exported by that module.

completionChecks.prompt: Why is import module java.base redundant in a compact source file?
choices: java.base is already imported for compact source files
java.base cannot be imported by any source file
List and Map are primitive types
completionChecks.explanation: Compact source files implicitly get java.base, so the declaration is useful here as an explicit teaching cue.

completionChecks.prompt: Does HashMap compile here without an explicit import java.util.HashMap declaration?
choices: Yes, because import module java.base imports public types from exported java.base packages
No, module imports expose only java.lang types
No, HashMap is not a public top-level type
Yes, but only because HashMap is a primitive wrapper
completionChecks.explanation: The trap is treating a module import like a single-package import; java.base exports java.util, so HashMap is in scope.
